text:
Please start requirements work on this feature:

> I want to add logging of the company_configuration that was set and used for a client per run. Add this to the devops display and storage in the automation runs collection.

The user just added this clarification — treat it as authoritative:

> "I am thinking like a button is shown on the top but next to each run and it will store it every time when we kick off a run. So we know what are all the config comes with that run no matter how people updating the config afterwards."

Key takeaways from the clarification:
1. **Snapshot semantics** — capture the company_configuration *at the moment the run is kicked off*. It must be immutable thereafter, even if someone edits the company_configuration later. The whole point is to know exactly which config was used for any historical run.
2. **Storage** — persist the snapshot on the automation runs collection record.
3. **Devops UI** — add a button next to each run (the user said "on the top but next to each run") that surfaces the captured config for that run. Decide whether that means a modal, drawer, expand, or a dedicated page — propose what fits the existing devops display patterns.

Please investigate the repo first (automation runs collection schema, where runs are kicked off, what `company_configuration` actually is, and the devops display layout), then send me (`team-lead`) any clarifying questions before writing the spec. I'll relay to the user. Once the spec is solid, hand off to `code-engineer`.
summary: Kick off — spec the company_config-per-run feature

text:
User answered your "set and used" question:

> **Config + per-run overrides** — also capture CLI flags, env-var overrides, and per-run params like `start_from_step` — anything that actually changed behavior for this run, not just the base company_configuration doc.

Please update the spec accordingly:
- Snapshot is no longer just `container.raw_config` — it needs to include whatever overrides/parameters the run was invoked with.
- Investigate what those override surfaces actually are in this codebase (CLI args, env vars read during run setup, per-run params on the pipeline orchestrator, anything else that mutates effective config between "the doc in Mongo" and "what the run actually executed against").
- The stored shape should make it clear what came from the base config vs. what was overridden — propose a structure (e.g. `{company_configuration: {...}, overrides: {...}}` or similar) so a user looking at the dialog can see both.
- Update the View Config dialog spec so the overrides are visible alongside the base config.
- The immutability and "snapshot at kickoff" semantics stay the same.

Please push the updated spec to `code-engineer` (they may have started — coordinate with them on Task #1 so they don't redo work that's still valid). Ping me when the engineer has the revised spec.

User confirmed both calls — no spec changes needed:

1. **Secrets stay excluded** — keep infra creds (`MONGODB_*_URI`, `PAYROC_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM`, etc.) out of the snapshot.
2. **The 4 overrides are the right set** — `ONLY_STEP`, `STOP_AFTER_STEP`, `ONLY_SCRIPT`, `PRESERVE_PREDICTIONS`. No additions.

Engineer can proceed as-is on Task #1. Ping me when the reviewer has reported.
summary: User confirmed — secrets out, 4 overrides good
